rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Hearing On Verified Petition To Release Property From Mechanics Lien
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 5, 2019 line 1. PETITIONER REALSMART FUND 23 L.P.'s Motion And Hearing On Verified Petition To Release Property From Mechanics Lien DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE. According to Civil Code 8488 (a), "[a]t the hearing both (1) the petition and (2) the issue of compliance with the service and date for hearing requirements of this article are deemed controverted by the claimant. The petitioner has the duty of producing evidence on those matters." (Ibid.) Petitioner failed to put forth evidence on compliance with CC 8484 (e), (g), (h). Additionally, the Court on its own motion pulled the petition in this case, and noticed that it was not verified and did not have the requisite declaration to satisfy CCP 8488. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: IGOR GAVRILOV ET AL VS. JAMIE CHANG ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 5, 2019 line 3. DEFENDANTs JAMIE CHANG and HIEN NGUYEN's MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION CONTINUED to April 15, 2019. (Parkview Villas Assn., Inc. v. State Farm Fire & Casualty Co. (2005) 133 Cal.App.4th 1197, 1211, 35 Cal.Rptr.3d 411. "[T]he proper response, in most instances, if the trial court is not prepared to address the merits of the motion in light of the deficient separate statement, is to give the opposing party an opportunity to file a proper separate statement....") Plaintiffs are to file a separate statement by April 5, 2019 at 5:00 pm. Moving party may file revised Reply, by 5:00 pm on April 9, 2019. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: JERRY H. KU VS. ANNA KU WONG ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Jerry H. Ku Notice Of Motion And Motion For Summary Judgment, Or Alternatively, Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 5, 2019 line 4. PLAINTIFF JERRY KU's Motion For Summary Judgment, Or Alternatively, Summary Adjudication is DENIED. Plaintiff Jerry H. Ku's motion for summary adjudication is DENIED. Plaintiff failed to comply with CRC 3.1350(b)(". . .If summary adjudication is sought, whether separately or as an alternative to the motion for summary judgment, the specific cause of action, affirmative defense, claims for damages, or issues of duty must be stated specifically in the notice of motion and be repeated, verbatim, in the separate statement of undisputed material facts"). Plaintiff Jerry H. Ku's motion for summary judgement is DENIED. The court finds multiple triable issue of material fact as to the elements of adverse possession. The disputed material facts include, but are not limited to: Whether Plaintiff had a good faith belief that he was the sole owner of the subject property during the requisite time period despite the letter from attorney John Preston expressly advising on p.2, paragraph 2, that "any property transferred to your Trust will become community property to give you and your spouse the income tax advantages which community property has over joint tenancy." (Exhbit D to Ku Declaration.) Said transfer of Plaintiff's interest from joint tenancy to community property was subsequently made (Exhibit C to the Ku Declaration). This creates a triable issue of fact with respect to Plaintiff's belief of sole ownership of the property. (Jerry Ku Declaration 4.) The Court also finds a triable issue of fact as to whether Plaintiff ever engaged in behavior that was so open, notorious and unequivocal in character that Defendants were given notice of intention to oust his familial co-tenants. (See Hacienda Ranch Homes, Inc. v. Superior Court, (2011) 198 Cal.App.4th 1122,1128.) = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: HOWARD C. GREENE, INC. VS. EUROMOTORS, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Each Affirmative Defense Alleged In Defendant Euromotors Answer To Plaintiffs Unlawful Detainer Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 5, 2019 line 6. PLAINTIFF HOWARD C. GREENE, INC.'s Motion To Strike Each Affirmative Defense Alleged In Defendant Euromotors Answer To Plaintiffs Unlawful Detainer Complaint GRANTED as to each affirmative defense with leave to amend. In unlawful detainers cases, affirmative defenses may be pleaded only to the extent they might defeat the landlord's right to possession. (Vella v. Hudgins, (1977) 20 Cal. 3d 251, 255 (superseded on other grounds by constitutional amendment as stated in Orcilla v. Big Sur, Inc., (2016) 244 Cal. App. 4th 982, 1011-1012; See e.g., Green v. Sup.Ct., (1974) 10 Cal.3d 616, 632-633 (warranty of habitability defense); Schweiger v. Sup.Ct. (1970) 3 Cal.3d 507, 517 (retaliatory eviction defense)]. Plaintiff may add facts/affirmative defenses that are limited to defeating the landlord's right to possession. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: HOWARD C. GREENE, INC. VS. EUROMOTORS, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO AMENDED ANSWER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 5, 2019 line 6. PLAINTIFF HOWARD C. GREENE, INC.'s DEMURRER TO AMENDED ANSWER TO COMPLAINT Plaintiff's General Demurrer is OVERULED. Defendant's Amended Complaint states facts sufficient to legally sustain a defense of improper notice under CCP section 1161(2). Plaintiff's Special Demurrer is OVERULED. = (501/CFH)
